args: "authorised config materialisation: the fetched secret is written into exactl...

declassifications.reason: authorised config materialisation: the fetched secret is written into exactly the output slot the config template declared for it, and nowhere else; this is the sole sanctioned secret-to-output crossing in the broker

functions.name: _capa_m7__hex_digit
functions.doc: One lowercase hex character for a nibble in 0..15.

functions.name: to_hex
functions.doc: Encode a byte list as a lowercase hex String, two chars per byte.

functions.name: _capa_m6__rotr32
functions.doc:
Rotate a 32-bit word right by `n` bits. `x` is assumed already
masked to 32 bits; `(x << (32 - n))` can reach 2^63 only if x had
high bits, so the whole expression is re-masked to 32 bits.

functions.name: _capa_m6__shr32
functions.doc: Logical right shift of an already-masked 32-bit word.

functions.name: _capa_m6__round_constants
functions.doc:
The 64 round constants (first 32 bits of the fractional parts of
the cube roots of the first 64 primes).

functions.name: _capa_m6__iv256
functions.doc: SHA-256 initial hash value.

functions.name: _capa_m6__iv224
functions.doc: SHA-224 initial hash value.

functions.name: _capa_m6__read_word
functions.doc:
Read one 32-bit big-endian word from `block` starting at byte `i`.
Each byte is in 0..255; the top byte is masked to 8 bits before
the 24-bit shift so the product stays well under i64.

functions.name: _capa_m6__schedule
functions.doc: Build the 64-word message schedule for one 64-byte block.

functions.name: _capa_m6__compress
functions.doc:
One block of the compression function. Returns the updated state
(the eight working words added back into the incoming state).

functions.name: _capa_m6__pad
functions.doc:
SHA padding: append 0x80, then zeros, then the 64-bit big-endian
bit length, to a multiple of 64 bytes. Returns a fresh byte list.

functions.name: _capa_m6__digest_words
functions.doc:
Run the full hash over `message` starting from `iv`, returning the
eight 32-bit state words.

functions.name: _capa_m6__slice64
functions.doc: Copy a 64-byte block starting at `start`.

functions.name: _capa_m6__words_to_bytes
functions.doc: Serialize `count` state words as big-endian bytes (4 each).

functions.name: sha256_bytes
functions.doc:
SHA-256 digest of `message` as 32 raw bytes.
`message`: bytes, each in 0..255; out-of-range elements are
silently masked to 8 bits (`& 0xFF`). See the BYTE CONTRACT above.

functions.name: sha256_hex
functions.doc:
SHA-256 digest of `message` as a 64-character lowercase hex String.
`message`: bytes, each in 0..255; out-of-range elements are
silently masked to 8 bits (`& 0xFF`). See the BYTE CONTRACT above.

functions.name: sha256_hex_utf8
functions.doc: SHA-256 of a String's UTF-8 bytes, as lowercase hex.

functions.name: sha224_bytes
functions.doc:
SHA-224 digest of `message` as 28 raw bytes (the first seven
state words; the eighth is dropped).
`message`: bytes, each in 0..255; out-of-range elements are
silently masked to 8 bits (`& 0xFF`). See the BYTE CONTRACT above.

functions.name: sha224_hex
functions.doc:
SHA-224 digest of `message` as a 56-character lowercase hex String.
`message`: bytes, each in 0..255; out-of-range elements are
silently masked to 8 bits (`& 0xFF`). See the BYTE CONTRACT above.

functions.name: sha224_hex_utf8
functions.doc: SHA-224 of a String's UTF-8 bytes, as lowercase hex.

operator_declared_grants.note: Authority declared by the operator at build/run time (e.g. --preopen). DISTINCT from the compiler-derived, program-proven capability surface; the compiler could not derive these grants.

compiler_derived_path_arg_surface.note: argv (env.args()) arguments the compiler PROVED reach an Fs / Net / Env sink, and whether read or write. A sound over-approximation (no reaching argument omitted; arg_index '*' when the concrete index is not statically determinate). Closures are covered SOUND BY CONSTRUCTION: a closure whose parameter reaches a sink is reported at '*' UNLESS the compiler proves it is applied only locally to non-argv values -- so a closure that escapes its frame (returned, passed to a helper, stored in an aggregate, or reached through a match/if arm or a name bound to one) fails closed, with no per-shape enumeration. Scope is EXHAUSTIVELY covered: the statement traversal descends into EVERY sub-scope that holds sub-statements -- if/while/for statement blocks, match-arm blocks, and lambda bodies, at any nesting depth -- so a binding or sink in any sub-block (including a match-arm block) is never skipped (pinned by a meta-test over the AST node inventory). RESIDUAL (the one honest gap, VALUE-FLOW only): a closure carried by a value the AST-level pass cannot statically tie back to a lambda -- re-extracted from a runtime container by key, or threaded through an opaque computed value -- may be under-reported. This does NOT grant authority -- a dynamic Fs/Net path still fail-closes in --wasi without --preopen; it is an auditable fact, distinct from operator_declared_grants.
